Package io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.transport

Class AbstractStreamTransport

java.lang.Object
io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.transport.AbstractTransport
io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.transport.AbstractStreamTransport
All Implemented Interfaces:
Transport, AutoCloseable
Direct Known Subclasses:
TcpTransport, TlsTransport
public abstract class AbstractStreamTransport extends AbstractTransport
Shared skeleton for all stream-oriented Transport implementations (TCP, TLS). Subclasses supply the socket via openSocket(); this class owns the reader thread, the write lock, and the socket-shutdown-first close protocol on top of the lifecycle skeleton provided by AbstractTransport.

Framing is delegated to StreamFramer, which enforces the 2-byte little-endian length prefix and the StreamFramer.MAX_FRAME_BYTES hard wire cap.

Message-oriented transports (WebSocket) do NOT extend this class — WebSocket already delimits messages so StreamFramer is not applicable. They extend AbstractTransport directly.

Threading contract inherited by every stream subclass:

  • doSend(byte[]) runs under a per-instance monitor so concurrent publishers serialise cleanly on the same socket.
  • The reader thread is a daemon; it never blocks JVM shutdown.
  • doClose() shuts input FIRST so the reader unblocks with EOFException or SocketException, then joins with a short timeout. Input-first is deliberate — closing the socket without shutting input first can leave the reader blocked on some Linux + JDK combinations.

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractStreamTransport

      public AbstractStreamTransport()

  • Method Details

    • openSocket

      protected abstract Socket openSocket() throws IOException
      Open the underlying stream socket. Called exactly once from doConnect(). Implementations are responsible for connect timeout, TCP tunables, TLS handshake, and any other transport-specific setup. Must return a fully connected socket.
      Throws:
      IOException

    • readerThreadTag

      protected abstract String readerThreadTag()
      Reader-thread name suffix; typically the endpoint (e.g. "tcp-127.0.0.1:7447").

    • doConnect

      protected final void doConnect() throws TransportException
      Description copied from class: AbstractTransport
      Open the underlying link. Called exactly once by AbstractTransport.connect() under the once-only guard. Subclasses arrange for received batches to be delivered via AbstractTransport.deliver(byte[]), and for asynchronous failures to be reported via AbstractTransport.reportReaderError(Throwable).
      Specified by:
      doConnect in class AbstractTransport
      Throws:
      TransportException - on connect failure (opened flag is cleared by AbstractTransport.connect() before rethrow)

    • doSend

      protected final void doSend(byte[] batch) throws TransportException
      Description copied from class: AbstractTransport
      Write one batch synchronously. Called under the caller thread; the caller does NOT hold any internal lock at this point. Subclasses may serialise on their own writer or rely on their transport primitive being thread-safe.
      Specified by:
      doSend in class AbstractTransport
      Throws:
      TransportException

    • doClose

      protected final void doClose()
      Description copied from class: AbstractTransport
      Release resources. Called at most once; the atomic-CAS in AbstractTransport.close() guarantees no re-entrance.
      Specified by:
      doClose in class AbstractTransport
